name: Fried Potatoes with Poblano Pepper Strips
recipeIngredient: 2 pounds red potatoes, sliced into 1/4-inch thick rounds
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the vegetable oil in a deep sauté pan so it reaches no more than one-third of the way up the sides of the pan. Heat the oil until a deep-fry thermometer registers 360 degrees.
2. Over an open flame, carefully char the Poblano peppers until the skin blisters and blackens. Set aside to cool.
3. When cooled, remove the seeds from the peppers and slice into thin strips.
4. In a large non-stick skillet, heat 3 tablespoons of oil over medium heat. Add the onion and cook until softened.
5. Once the oil is hot, carefully add the potatoes in batches, cooking and stirring for about 5 minutes or until tender, golden brown, and crispy. Cook the potatoes for 1 minute after adding them to the pan with the onions.
6. Drain well on paper towels and return the potatoes to the skillet. Add the pepper strips and season with salt and pepper.
7. Top with the grated Monteray Jack cheese and cook for an additional 1 minute until the cheese is melted and bubbly.
8. Transfer to a serving bowl and serve immediately.
Yield: 4 servings
